feedback_examples.strengths: Good use of functions to organize code. Variable names are clear and descriptive.
feedback_examples.improvements: Remember to use === instead of ==. Check array bounds to avoid off-by-one errors.
feedback_examples.overall_comment: Great start! Keep practicing and pay attention to edge cases.

feedback_examples.strengths: Logic is correct and handles basic cases well.
feedback_examples.improvements: Add input validation. Use const for values that don't change. Consider using array methods like map or filter.
feedback_examples.overall_comment: Solid work. Try refactoring to use modern JavaScript features.

feedback_examples.strengths: Code is well-structured with proper indentation. Good use of comments.
feedback_examples.improvements: Be careful with type coercion. Always declare variables with let or const. Test with edge cases like empty arrays.
feedback_examples.overall_comment: Excellent effort! Your code organization is good, focus on JavaScript-specific details next time.
